question_text: What does it mean when the forces on an object are balanced?

options.label: A
options.text: The forces are all equal in size

options.label: B
options.text: The forces cancel each other out and there is no net force

options.label: C
options.text: There is only one force acting on the object

correct_answer: The forces cancel each other out and there is no net force
explanation: When forces on an object are balanced, they have equal size but act in opposite directions. This means they cancel each other out, resulting in zero net force. The object will either remain stationary or continue moving at constant velocity.
